clusters.title: Internal Demo Preparation & Owner Assignments
clusters.summary: A cluster of notes from a product sync meeting focusing on preparing for an internal demo. It includes owner assignments for specific tasks (fixtures, signing flow, copy), instructions to limit the demo scope to a clear queue->batch->topic flow, a link to the demo script Linear ticket, and contingency planning for a recording.
keyPoints: Friday demo must show a clear queue->batch->topic flow, hiding extra UI complexity.
Owner assignments: Lou (seed fixtures), Chen (signing flow), Mia (settings copy).
Do not expand the /ai page; keep the demo focused on the result chain.
An 8-minute demo script and a fallback recording are needed in case of permission issues.
Invite 5 testers and have Chen record a clean run for polish.
clusters.reason: All captures are clearly part of a single, time-bound product coordination meeting focused on demo preparation and task assignments for an upcoming release. They share a common context of execution planning and reference each other (e.g., the demo flow, the script, the recording). The 'Weekly Product Ops' topic is a perfect fit for recurring meeting notes, owner assignments, and release coordination.

possibleTopics.reason: Directly matches the content about meeting coordination, owner assignments, and demo/release preparation.

promptText.system:
You are Tino's background batch compiler.
Return one JSON object only.
Do not use markdown fences.
Do not add explanation before or after the JSON.
The JSON must match this shape exactly:
{ "clusters": [{ "clusterId": string, "sourceIds": string[], "decision": "archive_to_topic" | "send_to_inbox" | "discard", "topicSlugSuggestion": string | null, "topicNameSuggestion": string | null, "title": string, "summary": string, "keyPoints": string[], "tags": string[], "confidence": number, "reason": string, "possibleTopics": [{ "topicSlug": string, "topicName": string, "reason": string | null }], "missingContext": string[] }] }

Important output rules
1. Output valid JSON only.
2. Use only sourceIds from the captures above.
3. Do not repeat sourceIds across clusters.
4. Assign every sourceId exactly once.
5. Use null when topic suggestion is unavailable.
6. Include missingContext and possibleTopics arrays even when empty.
